message:
fix(alloc,fuse,ondisk,cli): harden safety across allocation casts, FUSE offset validation, extent checksums, and xattr parsing

ffs-alloc:
- Replaced three dangerous `as u32` truncation casts with safe alternatives:
  from_superblock group_count uses u32::try_from().unwrap_or(u32::MAX)
  with saturation; free_inode and free_inode_persist group_idx use
  u32::try_from() with proper FfsError::Corruption error propagation

ffs-fuse:
- Replaced three u64::try_from(offset).unwrap_or(0) calls (read, readdir,
  write) with early-return EINVAL on negative offsets, with tracing warnings
  instead of silently treating negative offsets as zero

ffs-ondisk (ext4):
- validate_superblock: GDT offset now returns ParseError instead of
  silently using u64::MAX as fallback
- verify_extent_block_checksum: tail_off calculation hardened with
  checked_mul/checked_add to prevent overflow on malicious eh_max
- Ext4Inode::parse: added i_extra_isize bounds validation (must not
  extend beyond inode boundary)
- Ext4DirEntry.rec_len widened from u16 to u32 to support full
  block-sized records per ext4 kernel convention
- parse_xattr_entries now takes a separate value_base parameter, fixing
  value offset resolution for external xattr blocks
- Updated parse_ibody_xattrs, parse_xattr_block, and reader methods to
  pass correct value base slices
- Dir iteration: all-zero block now correctly yields no entries
  (rec_len=0 maps to block_size); added dir_iter_rec_len_too_small test
- Updated xattr test calls to match new two-argument signature

ffs-cli:
- DumpDirEntryOutput.rec_len type changed from u16 to u32 to match
  updated Ext4DirEntry struct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
